title: Claude Code Hook Envelope v1
description: Documents the JSON envelope Claude Code sends on stdin to cortina when a hook fires. This is the consumer-side contract: cortina reads these fields, Claude Code is the producer. No schema_version is present because Claude Code does not include one. Producer: Claude Code runtime. Consumer: cortina/src/adapters/claude_code.rs.

hook_event_name.description: Name of the hook that fired (e.g. PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact).

session_id.description: Claude Code session identifier. Present on most hooks.

cwd.description: Working directory of the agent process at the time the hook fired.

transcript_path.description: Absolute path to the session transcript JSONL file. Present on Stop and UserPromptSubmit hooks.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool that was called. Present on PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks.

tool_input.description: Tool-specific input arguments. Shape varies by tool_name.

file_path.description: Target file path (Write, Edit, MultiEdit tools).

tool_output.description: Tool-specific output. Present on PostToolUse hooks.

prompt.description: The user prompt text. Present on UserPromptSubmit hooks.

trigger.description: Compaction trigger reason (e.g. 'manual', 'auto'). Present on PreCompact hooks.

custom_instructions.description: Custom system instructions for the compacted session. Present on PreCompact hooks.
